artifact: apps/video-storefront — OneReel video storefront, full lifecycle cycle

hard_gates.detail: Intent ('smallest possible fix to the broken patronage act') compared to outcome (7-route operable app, 13.0% synthetic conversion, 3.8% refund rate vs 15% guardrail, all nonclaims preserved).
Decision: CONTINUE. Product is operable and spec-compliant at synthetic scope. Two P1 UX gaps deferred. Real traffic required for CTA experiment resolution.

dimensions.notes: Wins, failures, and unknowns explicitly separated. P1 gaps documented. CTA experiment explicitly inconclusive, not claimed as signal.
13.0% vs 14.6% theoretical — treated as within synthetic noise, not success claim. Refund 3.8% vs 15% guardrail — informational. Nonclaim preserved.
All 4 failures have specific root causes. No vague language. CTA experiment root cause correctly identifies PRNG copy-agnosticism.
CONTINUE justified: spec-compliant, all stages verified, P1 gaps explicitly deferred with rationale, CTA verdict ITERATE with clear prerequisite (real traffic).

non_claims: does not prove real payment processing
does not prove real patron behavior or conversion rates
synthetic cohort only — KPIs do not predict real traffic
CONTINUE decision applies to synthetic lifecycle only — real deployment requires provider access validation
